intro: Polypleurum elongatum, also known as Long-leaved Polypleurum, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Polypleurum elongatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Polypleurum elongatum include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
